from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Convert a list of triples about an athlete into a single fluent sentence."""
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Simple mapping from predicate to a natural‑language fragment.
    # Helper to strip surrounding quotes from objects (e.g., "\"Los Angeles Rams\"")
    def _clean(obj: str) -> str:
        return obj.strip().strip('"').strip("'")

    phrase_map = {
        "birthDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born on {o}",
        "birthPlace": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born in {o}",
        "birthYear": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born in {o}",
        "height": lambda s, o: f"{s} is {o} metres tall",
        "weight": lambda s, o: f"{s} weighs {o}",
        "club": lambda s, o: f"{s} plays for {o}",
        "currentclub": lambda s, o: f"{s} currently plays for {o}",
        "formerTeam": lambda s, o: f"{s} formerly played for {o}",
        "position": lambda s, o: f"{s} plays as a {o}",
        "league": lambda s, o: f"{s} competes in the {o} league",
        "manager": lambda s, o: f"{s} is managed by {o}",
        "coach": lambda s, o: f"{s} is coached by {o}",
        "ground": lambda s, o: f"{s}'s home ground is {o}",
        "stadium": lambda s, o: f"{s}'s stadium is {o}",
        "city": lambda s, o: f"{s} is based in {o}",
        "leader": lambda s, o: f"{s} is led by {o}",
        "anthem": lambda s, o: f"The national anthem of {s} is \"{o}\"",
        "ethnicGroup": lambda s, o: f"{s} includes the ethnic group {o}",
        "language": lambda s, o: f"The official language of {s} is {o}",
        "generalManager": lambda s, o: f"{s} has {o} as its general manager",
        "chairman": lambda s, o: f"{s} is chaired by {o}",
        "season": lambda s, o: f"The season for {s} was {o}",
        "currency": lambda s, o: f"The currency of {s} is {o}",
        "demonym": lambda s, o: f"The demonym for {s} is {o}",
        "officialLanguage": lambda s, o: f"The official language of {s} is {o}",
        "universityTeam": lambda s, o: f"{s} represents {o}",
        "mostChampions": lambda s, o: f"The most championships belong to {o} in {s}",
        "leaderTitle": lambda s, o: f"The leader title of {s} is {o}",
        "isPartOf": lambda s, o: f"{s} is part of {o}",
        "country": lambda s, o: f"{s} is located in {o}",
        "utcOffset": lambda s, o: f"The UTC offset of {s} is {o}",
        "timeZone": lambda s, o: f"The time zone of {s} is {o}",
        "areaTotal": lambda s, o: f"The total area of {s} is {o}",
        "foundingDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} was founded on {o}",
        "draftYear": lambda s, o: f"{s} was drafted in {o}",
        "draftRound": lambda s, o: f"{s} was selected in round {o}",
        "draftPick": lambda s, o: f"{s} was the {o}th pick",
        "college": lambda s, o: f"{s} attended {o}",
        "youthclub": lambda s, o: f"{s} started at youth club {o}",
        "playerNumber": lambda s, o: f"{s} wears number {o}",
        "activeYearsStartYear": lambda s, o: f"{s} started his professional career in {o}",
        "debutTeam": lambda s, o: f"{s} debuted with {o}",
        "currentteam": lambda s, o: f"{s} currently plays for {o}",
        "draftTeam": lambda s, o: f"{s} was drafted by {o}",
        # Fallback for any other predicate
        "default": lambda s, p, o: f"{s} {p} {o}",
    }

    # Group triples by subject to enable richer natural language merging.
    subject_map = {}
    for triple in triples:
        subject_map.setdefault(triple.subject, []).append(triple)

    # Helper to create a phrase for a single predicate/object.
    def _phrase(subj: str, pred: str, obj: str) -> str:
        formatter = phrase_map.get(pred, None)
        if formatter:
            try:
                return formatter(subj, obj)
            except TypeError:
                return phrase_map["default"](subj, pred, obj)
        else:
            return phrase_map["default"](subj, pred, obj)

    # Build a sentence fragment for each subject, merging related predicates.
    subject_clauses = []
    for subj, triples_list in subject_map.items():
        # Organise predicates for this subject.
        pred_objs = {}
        for t in triples_list:
            pred_objs.setdefault(t.predicate, []).append(_clean(t.object))

        # Special handling for birthDate + birthPlace combination.
        if "birthDate" in pred_objs and "birthPlace" in pred_objs:
            date = pred_objs["birthDate"][0]
            place = pred_objs["birthPlace"][0]
            clause = f"{subj} was born on {date} in {place}"
            # Remove used predicates.
            del pred_objs["birthDate"]
            del pred_objs["birthPlace"]
        else:
            clause = ""

        # Create phrases for remaining predicates.
        other_phrases = []
        for pred, objs in pred_objs.items():
            # Join multiple objects with commas and 'and'.
            if len(objs) == 1:
                obj_str = objs[0]
            else:
                obj_str = ", ".join(objs[:-1]) + f", and {objs[-1]}"
            other_phrases.append(_phrase(subj, pred, obj_str))

        # Combine the birth clause with other phrases.
        if clause:
            if other_phrases:
                clause = f"{clause}, and " + ", ".join(other_phrases)
        else:
            clause = ", ".join(other_phrases)

        subject_clauses.append(clause.rstrip(", "))

    # Combine all subject clauses into a single fluent sentence.
    if not subject_clauses:
        return ""
    if len(subject_clauses) == 1:
        sentence = subject_clauses[0] + "."
    else:
        sentence = "; ".join(subject_clauses) + "."
    return sentence

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
